title: Session Lifecycle Management
description: Manage session lifecycle with pruning, TTL configuration, tagging, usage statistics, and cross-project queries. Extends existing session capture infrastructure from epic 010.

title: Foundational: Extend session types and clients
description: WHY: All user stories require extensions to the session type system (tags field), config schema (TTL), and storage/metadata clients (delete operations). These must be in place before any story can proceed.
design: Files: pkg/cli/session/types.go (add Tags field to SessionMetadata), pkg/cli/session/metadata.go (add Delete method), pkg/cli/session/storage.go (add Delete method), pkg/cli/metadata/schema.go (add TTL field to ProjectMetadata)

title: US1: Prune Old Sessions
description: WHY: Storage grows indefinitely without cleanup. Users need to remove sessions older than a threshold to reclaim storage and keep session lists clean.
design: Add sl session prune command with --days, --dry-run, --expired flags. Implement prune logic that deletes both cloud storage objects and metadata entries. Requires auth.

title: US2: Configure Session TTL
description: WHY: Project maintainers need to set retention policy so expired sessions can be pruned consistently across team members.
design: Add session.ttl_days field to specledger.yaml schema. Integrate with prune --expired mode. Default 30 days.

title: US3: Tag Sessions for Organization
description: WHY: Developers need to categorize sessions for discoverability. Tags from branch names and manual input help organize sessions.
design: Add Tags field to SessionMetadata, auto-tag from branch in capture.go, --tag flag on capture, --tag filter on list. Files: types.go, capture.go, metadata.go, commands/session.go

title: US4: View Session Usage Statistics
description: WHY: Developers and team leads need aggregate metrics about session usage for capacity planning and cost justification.
design: Add sl session stats command. Query metadata for aggregate metrics: total count, total storage, per-branch distribution, message count averages, date range. File: commands/session.go

title: US5: Query Sessions Across Projects
description: WHY: Developers working across multiple repos want to find context from past sessions regardless of project.
design: Add --all-projects flag to sl session list. Query metadata without project_id filter. Requires auth. File: commands/session.go, metadata.go

title: Polish: Cross-cutting concerns and hardening
description: WHY: After all stories are implemented, cross-cutting concerns like error handling consistency, offline queue TTL awareness, and edge case handling need attention.

title: Add Tags field to SessionMetadata type
description: WHY: All tag-related features (US3) and future filtering need a tags field on session metadata.

items.item: JSON tag includes omitempty for backward compatibility

design: File: pkg/cli/session/types.go. Add Tags []string json:tags to SessionMetadata struct. Update any constructors or factory functions.
acceptance_criteria: SessionMetadata has Tags []string field. Existing serialization is backward-compatible (empty tags = omitempty).

title: Add Delete method to storage client
description: WHY: Pruning (US1) needs to remove session files from Supabase Storage.

items.item: Delete method added to storage client

design: File: pkg/cli/session/storage.go. Add Delete(storagePath string) error method. Use HTTP DELETE to /storage/v1/object/{bucket}/{storagePath} with auth headers.
acceptance_criteria: Storage client can delete objects by path. Returns error on failure.

title: Add Delete method to metadata client
description: WHY: Pruning (US1) needs to remove session metadata records from Supabase PostgREST.

items.item: Delete method added to metadata client

design: File: pkg/cli/session/metadata.go. Add Delete(sessionID string) error method. Use HTTP DELETE to /rest/v1/sessions?id=eq.{sessionID} with auth headers.
acceptance_criteria: Metadata client can delete session records by ID. Returns error on failure.

title: Add TTL field to project config schema
description: WHY: TTL configuration (US2) needs a persistent setting in specledger.yaml for session retention policy.

items.item: TTL field added to config schema
Default value of 30 days applied when field is missing

design: File: pkg/cli/metadata/schema.go. Add SessionTTLDays int to ProjectInfo or a new Session struct. Default 30 days. File: pkg/cli/metadata/yaml.go - ensure Load/Save handles the new field.
acceptance_criteria: specledger.yaml can store session.ttl_days value. Default is 30 when not set.

title: Implement session prune logic
description: WHY: Core pruning logic needs to identify expired sessions and delete both storage and metadata.

items.item: Deletes both storage and metadata per session
Dry-run mode lists candidates without deleting

design: File: pkg/cli/session/prune.go (new). PruneOptions struct with DaysOld int, DryRun bool, UseExpired bool, ProjectID string. PruneSessions function: query metadata by date filter, iterate sessions, delete storage + metadata for each. Handle partial failures (continue on error, report counts). Requires auth token.
acceptance_criteria: Prune identifies sessions older than threshold. Deletes both storage object and metadata entry. Dry-run lists without deleting. Partial failures reported with success/fail counts.

title: Add sl session prune CLI command
description: WHY: Users need a CLI command to invoke pruning with options for age threshold, dry-run, and expired mode.

items.item: prune subcommand added to session command
Auth check with helpful error message

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/session.go. Add pruneCmd subcommand with flags: --days int (default 30), --dry-run bool, --expired bool. Calls prune.PruneSessions. Outputs results table or dry-run list. Requires auth via auth.GetValidAccessToken().
acceptance_criteria: sl session prune --days 30 deletes old sessions. --dry-run shows what would be deleted. --expired uses configured TTL. Unauthenticated users see auth error.

title: Add sl session config TTL CLI command
description: WHY: Users need to view and set session TTL via CLI.

items.item: TTL read from specledger.yaml when --expired used
Default 30 days when TTL not configured

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/session.go. Add config subcommand or integrate TTL into prune --expired. When --expired is used, read TTL from specledger.yaml via metadata.LoadFromProject(). Explicit --days overrides configured TTL (FR-005).
acceptance_criteria: Prune --expired uses configured TTL. Explicit --days overrides TTL. Default TTL is 30 days when not configured.

title: Auto-tag sessions from branch name during capture
description: WHY: Sessions should be automatically categorized by branch naming patterns for discoverability.

items.item: Tag extraction from branch name implemented

design: File: pkg/cli/session/capture.go. Extract tag from branch name patterns: feature/xxx -> xxx, fix/xxx -> xxx, 608-session-lifecycle -> session-lifecycle. Add extracted tags to SessionContent/Metadata.Tags. Deduplicate tags.
acceptance_criteria: Branch feature/user-auth produces tag user-auth. Branch 608-session-lifecycle produces tag session-lifecycle. Duplicate tags deduplicated.

title: Add --tag flag to session capture command
description: WHY: Users need to manually add custom tags when capturing sessions.

items.item: Tag flag added to capture command

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/session.go. Add --tag string slice flag to capture subcommand. Merge custom tags with auto-generated tags. Pass to capture logic.
acceptance_criteria: User can pass --tag auth --tag feature to capture. Custom tags merged with auto-generated tags. Duplicates removed.

title: Add --tag filter to session list command
description: WHY: Users need to filter sessions by tag to find categorized sessions quickly.

items.item: Tag filter flag added to list command
Multi-tag sessions appear for any matching tag

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/session.go. Add --tag string flag to list subcommand. File: pkg/cli/session/metadata.go - add tag filter to Query method using PostgREST array contains operator (cs.{tag}).
acceptance_criteria: sl session list --tag auth shows only sessions tagged with auth. Sessions with multiple tags appear when filtering by any one tag.

title: Implement sl session stats command
description: WHY: Users need aggregate metrics about session usage for capacity planning and cost justification.

items.item: Total count and storage size displayed
Missing metadata fields warned and skipped

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/session.go. Add stats subcommand. Query all sessions for project, compute: total count, total storage size, per-branch distribution (count + size), average message count, date range (oldest to newest). Handle empty state. Output with tabwriter.
acceptance_criteria: Stats show total count, storage size, per-branch distribution, message averages, date range. No sessions = clear message. Missing metadata fields handled gracefully with warnings.

title: Add --all-projects flag to session list
description: WHY: Developers working across repos want to find session context regardless of which project they were working on.

items.item: all-projects flag added to list command
Metadata query supports no project_id filter

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/session.go. Add --all-projects bool flag to list subcommand. When set, query metadata without project_id filter. Requires auth. File: pkg/cli/session/metadata.go - support optional project_id in Query.
acceptance_criteria: sl session list --all-projects returns sessions from all accessible projects. Requires authentication. Unauthenticated users see auth error.

title: Offline queue TTL-aware discard
description: WHY: Expired queued sessions should be discarded rather than uploaded, saving bandwidth and storage.

items.item: Expired entries discarded with log message

design: File: pkg/cli/session/queue.go. In ProcessQueue, check each entry age against configured TTL. If expired, discard entry instead of uploading. Log discarded entries.
acceptance_criteria: Queued sessions older than TTL are discarded during sync. Discarded entries logged. Non-expired entries uploaded normally.

title: Add Supabase migration for tags column
description: WHY: The sessions table needs a tags column to store session tags in the database.

items.item: Tags column added as text array

design: File: supabase/migrations/20260311_add_session_tags.sql. ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN tags text[] DEFAULT ARRAY[]::text[]. Add GIN index for array contains queries.
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds tags column. Existing rows get empty array default. GIN index enables tag filtering.
